blurb: Delivers government services to people and businesses through Service BC, manages government digital services, data, procurement, and connectivity across the province.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: British Columbians can easily get help from government in person, by phone, and online
goals.description: Service BC is the front line for hundreds of provincial programs and services, helping people get ID, order life-event documents, and connect with the right part of government by phone, text, email, or in person.

supporting.name: Get help with government programs and services by phone, text, or email
supporting.desc: Service BC's contact centre answers general questions about provincial government programs and connects people to the right ministry or service, in over 220 languages, with accessible options including Video Relay Service and TDD.

supporting.name: Visit a Service BC location for in-person help
supporting.desc: Service BC offices across the province provide in-person help with hundreds of provincial programs and services, including ID, vital records, and business registrations.

main_services.name: Get government ID and life event documents

supporting.name: Get a BC Services Card
supporting.desc: The BC Services Card is government-issued photo ID used to access health services, verify identity, and set up a digital account to log in to online government services.

relationships.service: Enrol in the Medical Services Plan (MSP)

supporting.name: Renew or replace your BC Services Card
supporting.desc: Renew an expiring card, or replace a lost, stolen, or damaged BC Services Card.

supporting.name: Register your child's birth
supporting.desc: Register a newborn's birth online through the Electronic Birth Registration System, a legal requirement in British Columbia.

supporting.name: Order a birth, marriage, or death certificate
supporting.desc: Order certified copies of B.C. birth, marriage, and death certificates online through the Vital Statistics Online Certificate Ordering System.

supporting.name: Get a marriage licence
supporting.desc: Purchase a marriage licence before your wedding date, required to legally marry in British Columbia.

supporting.name: Change your legal name
supporting.desc: Apply for a legal change of name, or assume a spouse's last name after marriage.

supporting.name: Report a death and settle affairs after a death
supporting.desc: Guidance on what to do when someone dies in B.C., including registering the death and an after-death checklist for settling affairs.

main_services.name: Keep your information up to date with government

supporting.name: Update your address with multiple government organizations at once
supporting.desc: Address Change BC lets people moving within British Columbia update their address with several provincial and local government organizations in a single online transaction.
supporting.users: B.C. residents moving within the province

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: People and businesses have secure digital identity to access government services online
goals.description: The BC Services Card, BC Wallet, and BCeID give people and businesses a trusted way to prove who they are and log in to government services online, without a separate account for every site.

supporting.name: Set up a BC Services Card account for online services
supporting.desc: Create a BC Services Card digital account, using your physical BC Services Card, to log in to government and partner services online.

supporting.name: Get a BC Wallet or person credential
supporting.desc: BC Wallet is a mobile app that lets people hold and share verifiable digital credentials, including a Person Credential based on their BC Services Card.

supporting.name: Get a BCeID to access online government services
supporting.desc: A BCeID account provides secure sign-in to a range of provincial and municipal online services for individuals and businesses.

supporting.name: Log in to participating services with your BC Services Card
supporting.desc: Use BC Services Card Login to sign in to websites and services that accept it, without creating a separate username and password for each one.

channels.label: Websites that accept BC Services Card Login

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: Government information is open, protected, and accountable to the public
goals.description: The ministry administers freedom of information and privacy law, publishes open government information, and oversees data standards, including the Anti-Racism Data Act.

supporting.name: Request information about government activities
supporting.desc: Under the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act, anyone can request records about the activities or programs of a B.C. government ministry or agency.

legislation.full_name: Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act  -  Right of access to records

supporting.name: Request your personal information from government records
supporting.desc: Individuals can request their own personal information held in provincial government records, with no application fee.

legislation.full_name: Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act  -  Right of access to records

supporting.name: Request personal information for someone else
supporting.desc: Request personal information held by government on behalf of another person, for example as their legal representative.

channels.label: Request personal information for someone else

legislation.full_name: Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act  -  Right of access to records

supporting.name: Check the status of a freedom of information request
supporting.desc: Check on the progress of a submitted freedom of information request.

channels.label: Check the status of a request

supporting.name: Search the open information catalogue
supporting.desc: Search and browse government records that have already been released or are published proactively, before submitting a new FOI request.

supporting.name: View statistics on systemic racism and racial equity
supporting.desc: Under the Anti-Racism Data Act, government publishes annual statistics and research to help identify and eliminate systemic racism in public programs and services.
supporting.users: General public, researchers, Indigenous and racialized communities

legislation.full_name: Anti-Racism Data Act  -  Publication of statistics and research

goals.title: GOAL 4
goals.short_name: B.C.'s registries, procurement, and connectivity connect people and businesses to the economy
goals.description: BC Registries administers the legal framework for starting and searching businesses and other organizations, BC Bid and BC Auction connect suppliers and buyers with government, and connectivity programs expand high-speed internet to underserved communities.

main_services.name: Register and search a business or organization

supporting.name: Register a sole proprietorship or partnership
supporting.desc: Start, register, and maintain a sole proprietorship or general partnership through the BC Business Registry.

supporting.name: Incorporate or manage a company
supporting.desc: Incorporate a limited company or benefit company, file annual reports, and manage director and share information through Corporate Online and the BC Registry application.

legislation.full_name: Business Corporations Act  -  Incorporation and corporate filings

supporting.name: Register or maintain a not-for-profit society
supporting.desc: Incorporate, amalgamate, or maintain a not-for-profit society under the Societies Act through Societies Online.

supporting.name: Register or maintain a cooperative association
supporting.desc: Incorporate, dissolve, or maintain a cooperative association that provides goods or services such as housing, food, or health care to its members.

supporting.name: Search for business, lien, or manufactured home records
supporting.desc: Search registered businesses, personal property liens, and manufactured home records, or request certificates of status and good standing.

supporting.name: Bid on government contracts
supporting.desc: BC Bid is the online marketplace where public sector organizations advertise contract opportunities for goods, services, and construction, and suppliers submit bids.

supporting.name: Buy surplus government assets
supporting.desc: BC Auction sells used and surplus provincial government assets through online bidding, open to anyone with internet access.

supporting.name: Apply for connectivity infrastructure funding
supporting.desc: Connecting Communities BC funds projects that expand high-speed internet access to underserved households, with priority for rural, remote, and First Nations communities.
supporting.users: Internet service providers, local and Indigenous governments

supporting.name: Check internet and cellular coverage in your area
supporting.desc: Find out the current status of high-speed internet and cellular service availability across British Columbia.

foundational_services.name: Provide identity assurance and authentication infrastructure for digital government services
foundational_services.desc: Operates the BC Services Card Authentication Service and related identity infrastructure that lets other government services verify who a person is online.

foundational_services.name: Lead information technology strategy, policy, and shared infrastructure for government
foundational_services.desc: Sets IT strategy, policy, and standards for the province through Connected Services BC, provides IT infrastructure and shared services, and manages IT capital investment across government.
